title: 10 best herbal creams for over bleeding during periods

remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in Witch Hazel help to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and thus alleviating excessive menstrual bleeding.
Astringent Properties: The astringent properties of Witch Hazel help to dry up excess moisture and reduce swelling in the vaginal area, which can contribute to heavy bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory Compounds: Witch Hazel contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps associated with menstruation, which can exacerbate excessive bleeding.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American Witch Hazel, creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in the plant help to reduce bleeding and promote clotting by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the uterine lining. The cream also has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it a popular natural remedy for excessive menstrual bleeding and heavy periods. It can be used externally to reduce bleeding and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Hamamelitannin: A type of tannin, hamamelitannin has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
Oleanolic acid: A triterpenoid saponin, oleanolic acid has anti-inflammatory and astringent effects that may help to reduce inflammation and bleeding in the uterine lining.
Tannins (specifically Gallic acid): Tannins, including gallic acid, have astringent properties that help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and promoting coagulation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of tannins, which have astringent properties that help reduce bleeding.
Buds: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the uterine lining.
Barks: Employed for their tannin content, which helps to reduce bleeding and prevent infections.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Melt 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler and set aside for 10 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with 1/4 cup of shea butter and 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil in a separate bowl.
Combine the melted beeswax with the oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated and thickened.

remedy_properties: Astringent and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Pot marigold cream's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding and inflammation associated with heavy menstrual flow.

Antioxidant and Free Radical Scavenging Properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which may exacerbate heavy bleeding during periods, helping to protect the uterine lining from damage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's flavonoids and saponins help to reduce swelling and ease cramping, while its antimicrobial properties prevent infection and promote wound healing. Additionally, Calendula's ability to stimulate blood clotting and reduce menstrual flow makes it an effective natural remedy for excessive bleeding during periods, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and preventing fluid loss.

Flavonoids (especially Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid found in Calendula, has anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menorrhagia.
Phenolic Acids (especially Caffeic Acid): Caffeic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menorrhagia.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers contain triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce bleeding and promote healing.
Leaves: The leaves have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin, reducing bleeding and discomfort during menstruation.
Seeds: The seeds contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce bleeding and promote healing in the uterine lining.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula flowers when fully open and dry them in a dark place for 2 weeks to preserve their potency.
Infuse 1 cup of dried calendula flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 70-80 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax and 1-2 tablespoons of coconut oil to create a smooth consistency.
Whip the mixture until light and fluffy then pour it into jars and store them in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Stinging nettle creams contain astringent compounds that help constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and minimizing bleeding.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Vasoprotective properties: Nettle's vasoprotective compounds help strengthen and stabilize blood vessel walls, preventing them from rupturing or leaking excessively during menstruation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich content of iron and other essential nutrients. The cream is believed to help in regulating menstrual flow, reducing inflammation and soothing the uterine lining, thereby reducing excessive bleeding. The anti-inflammatory properties present in the cream also help in calming the uterine muscles, promoting a more balanced menstrual cycle. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh urtica dioica leaves and flowers from a clean and unpolluted area.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and dry them in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1-3 days.
Mix 1 cup of dried urtica dioica with 1 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20-30 minutes until it reaches a temperature of 180-190°f and thickens.
Strain the hot mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and let it cool completely.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of tilia platyphyllos leaves and flowers on a sunny day in july.
Dry the collected plant material in a warm room for 2 weeks at 30c.
Infuse 250g of dried tilia platyphyllos in 1000ml of cold-pressed oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and mix it with 100g of beeswax and 50g of candelilla wax.
Heat the mixture at 40c for 10 minutes to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax with 200 grams of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Add the vaccinium macrocarpon powder to the warm oil mixture and stir until well combined for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and stir in 20 grams of shea butter and 10 grams of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Weigh 250g of ginkgo biloba extract standardized to contain 24% flavone glycosides and 6% terpene lactones.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Mix 20g of shea butter and 10g of vitamin e oil with the melted beeswax mixture.
Add 2.5ml of distilled water to the mixture and stir in 2.5g of triethanolamine.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried chamomile flowers and measure out 2 tablespoons.

Steep the mixture for 4 hours at room temperature then strain it through cheesecloth.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and warm them together gently.
Combine the cooled chamomile infusion with the wax-oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers for one cup quantity and clean them properly.
Combine the cleaned herbs with carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil in a ratio of one part herb to two parts oil.
Heat the mixture at low temperature around 150-160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to 1 hour to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids then add vitamin e oil as a preservative in a dosage of five drops per ounce.
Transfer the extracted herbal infusion into clean glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for a minimum period of 2 weeks to allow the mixture to mature.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel seeds, clean them thoroughly, and grind into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground fennel powder with 100ml of coconut oil, 20ml of beeswax, and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a heat-resistant bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and gently heat it over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes until melted and well combined.
Remove the mixture from the heat, let it cool to room temperature, then whip it using an electric mixer or whisk for 5 minutes until creamy and light.
Transfer the cream to sterilized glass jars, label them with the date and ingredients, and store in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and dry them immediately using a food dehydrator or air drying method.
Combine 50 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to allow for optimal herbal extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass jar, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating gently until melted and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the uterine lining. The antispasmodic properties in lemon balm help to reduce inflammation and ease cramps, which can contribute to excessive bleeding. Additionally, lemon balm has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and promote clotting, reducing the amount of bleeding. By applying a topical cream containing lemon balm directly to the affected area, women may experience reduced bleeding and discomfort during their periods.

Seeds: Containing fatty acids and antioxidants that aid in soothing and calming the skin, reducing bleeding and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried melissa officinalis leaves from a trusted source or purchase them online for use in the recipe.
Grind 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered melissa officinalis with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax pellets in a double boiler setup for 10 minutes.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture has a smooth consistency taking approximately 15 minutes.
Remove from heat let cool slightly then pour into sterilized containers and store in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cape jasmine creams helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique combination of natural ingredients that promote uterine health and reduce menstrual flow. The anti-inflammatory properties in cape jasmine help to alleviate cramps, while its astringent properties constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing excessive bleeding. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties soothe the vaginal mucosa, preventing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate bleeding. This holistic approach helps to regulate menstrual flow, providing relief from the discomfort and inconvenience of heavy periods.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gardenia jasminoides flowers and leaves in the morning to ensure optimal potency for infusion.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Combine 20g of dried gardenia jasminoides with 100ml of cold pressed coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and let it cool to room temperature before refrigerating for 2 hours.
Whip 50g of unflavored yogurt with 10 drops of vitamin e oil and add the cooled infused oil mixture, whipping until smooth and creamy for application.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterus and alleviate heavy bleeding.
Antioxidants: The herb is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that can help protect the uterine lining from damage and oxidative stress, thereby reducing excessive bleeding.
Astringent properties: Purple coneflower has astringent properties, which can help contract the uterine muscles and reduce heavy bleeding by stimulating the production of prostaglandins.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the purple coneflower have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the uterine lining, reducing heavy bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves.
Combine the harvested material with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture for four weeks by storing it in a cool dark place, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid from the solids through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids and reserving 50 milliliters of the infused oil.
Mix the reserved infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and heat gently in a double boiler until melted and combined.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called arnica, which helps to reduce swelling in the uterine lining and ease menstrual cramping. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant properties help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing excessive bleeding. The natural ingredients also provide gentle warmth and comfort to the affected area, allowing for a more comfortable and manageable period experience.

Rosemary creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain bioactive compounds that have a natural ability to stimulate blood platelets, thus reducing excessive bleeding. The essential oils in rosemary, such as carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, leading to reduced bleeding. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves and dry them in shade for two weeks.
Grind the dried herbs into fine powder using mortar and pestle, then sift through a mesh strainer.
Mix 20 grams of the herb powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it gently for one hour.
Remove from heat and let it infuse for four hours before filtering the mixture through cheesecloth or cotton cloth into a clean glass jar.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter to the filtered liquid, melting them in a double boiler while stirring constantly for five minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and constriction that can lead to excessive bleeding. Additionally, thyme has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, promoting clotting and slowing down menstrual flow. By applying thyme cream topically, women can experience relief from heavy or prolonged bleeding, allowing for a more comfortable and manageable period.

remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain compounds that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
Buds: Thyme buds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers on a sunny morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Mix 20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris with 40 grams of sweet almond oil and heat it in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 10 grams of beeswax and stir until melted, then remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture using an electric mixer for 5 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for over bleeding during periods are topical remedies made from natural herbs that help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding and alleviate associated discomforts. These creams are formulated with specific herbs such as Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Hypericum perforatum, Cimicifuga racemosa, Trifolium pratense, Ginkgo biloba, Althaea officinalis, Vitex agnus-castus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Hamamelis virginiana. If left untreated, over bleeding can lead to fatigue, anxiety, and emotional distress, negatively impacting daily life, relationships, and work productivity, ultimately affecting overall well-being and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal creams are hemorrhoids, varicose veins, and eczema. These conditions can cause discomfort, itching, and inflammation, making them ideal candidates for natural treatments. Herbal creams containing ingredients like witch hazel, aloe vera, and tea tree oil can soothe and calm the affected areas, reducing pain and discomfort while promoting healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with over bleeding during periods is a blend of Calendula and Witch Hazel creams. Calendula cream is known for its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help reduce bleeding and pain. Witch Hazel cream, on the other hand, has astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. Applying a combination of these creams can provide relief from heavy bleeding and discomfort associated with menstrual periods. They can also help soothe and calm the vaginal area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work to soothe and protect the vaginal tissues. This reduces inflammation and promotes healing, helping to regulate menstrual flow. By reducing blood clotting and promoting coagulation, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate heavy bleeding and menstrual cramps, providing relief and comfort to individuals experiencing excessive bleeding during their periods.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, which can contribute to reducing excessive bleeding.

Phenolic acids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce uterine inflammation and promote clotting to reduce heavy bleeding.

Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene and lutein): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and promote tissue repair, contributing to reducing excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce bleeding and promote healing.
Leaves: They are used due to their ability to help regulate menstrual flow and reduce heavy bleeding.
Seeds: They are not typically used for this purpose, but the flowers and leaves are the most used parts for this specific issue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 flowers of calendula officinalis when they are in full bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 7-10 days to remove moisture completely.
Infuse 1 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter for moisturizing properties.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce menstrual flow and ease cramps. The cooling and moisturizing effects of aloe vera also help to calm the uterus and reduce inflammation, promoting a more balanced menstrual cycle and alleviating excessive bleeding. Regular use of aloe barbadensis cream may provide relief from menstrual discomfort and heavy bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and preventing excessive blood loss.
Glycoproteins: These mucilaginous compounds help to soothe and protect the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and preventing excessive bleeding during menstrual periods.

Vitamins and minerals (such as Vitamin A, E, and Iron): These nutrients help to promote healthy cell growth, reduce inflammation, and prevent anemia by replenishing iron stores, ultimately reducing excessive bleeding during menstrual periods.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are used due to their gel content, which is rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.

Gel from Leaves: The gel is extracted from the leaves, which is then used in creams and other products to provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help alleviate menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Weigh out 50 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and add 10 grams of distilled water in a bowl.
Blend the mixture on high speed for 2 minutes to break down the cell walls and release the gel's properties.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter with 10 grams of coconut oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture becomes smooth.
Add the blended aloe barbadensis mixture to the saucepan and stir well to combine the ingredients completely.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds can help reduce menstrual cramps and promote relaxation, which may slow down heavy bleeding. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum creams may help regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to excessive bleeding. By reducing inflammation and promoting hormonal balance, these creams may provide natural relief from heavy menstrual bleeding and associated discomfort. This can promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Acts as a uterine relaxant, reducing uterine contractions and thereby helping to control excessive menstrual bleeding.
Quercetin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress that can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Kaempferol: Has a potential role in regulating prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can cause the uterus to contract and lead to heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Flowers: Used due to their ability to reduce bleeding and inflammation.
Stems: Used due to their astringent properties and ability to contract blood vessels, reducing bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 35-40°c for 8-12 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 30-50 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 200-300 grams of cold-pressed oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 40-50°c for 6-8 hours while occasionally stirring to facilitate oil extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and transfer the resulting oil into a clean container for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a natural blend of extracts from the black cohosh plant, which has been used for centuries to regulate menstrual cycles. The creams work by toning the uterine muscles, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of hormones. As a result, they help to alleviate heavy bleeding, cramping, and discomfort associated with over bleeding during periods, promoting a more balanced and comfortable menstrual experience. This natural remedy is a popular alternative to synthetic medications.
remedy_constituents: Actein: Acts as a uterine relaxant, helping to reduce excessive uterine contractions that can cause heavy bleeding during menstruation.
Isoferulic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus, thereby alleviating heavy bleeding.

Rotundifuran: Has been shown to exhibit estrogen-like activity, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce excessive bleeding by promoting uterine relaxation and reducing prostaglandin production.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Cimicifuga racemosa are rich in actein, a compound that has a mild astringent effect, helping to reduce heavy bleeding during periods.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain a higher concentration of actein than the roots, making them a preferred choice for their astringent and hemostatic properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Cimicifuga racemosa contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, helping to reduce heavy bleeding and alleviate symptoms associated with over bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried cimicifuga racemosa roots and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Combine the cleaned cimicifuga racemosa roots with 100ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Gently heat the mixture for 2 hours at a temperature of 60-70°c to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal infusion.
Mix the herbal infusion with 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain the active compound isoflavones, which have a natural ability to stimulate the uterus and help it return to its normal state. This can help to reduce heavy bleeding by promoting clotting and reducing inflammation. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Trifolium pratense creams may also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of excessive menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, have been shown to have estrogenic activity, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus, leading to reduced bleeding.

Coumarins (Aesculin and Aesculetin): Coumarins have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and reducing inflammation in the uterine lining.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of isoflavones, which have a balancing effect on hormones and can help reduce menstrual bleeding.
Flowers: They are used for their estrogenic properties, which can help regulate the menstrual cycle and reduce heavy bleeding.
Roots: They are used due to their content of isoflavones and other phytoestrogens, which can help stabilize hormone levels and alleviate menstrual issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh or dried trifolium pratense flowers and leaves, clean and chop them finely.
Infuse 1 cup of chopped trifolium pratense in 2 cups of carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids, then add 10% beeswax to 90% infused oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 120-140 degrees fahrenheit.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify completely before use, approximately 30 minutes to 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, which can contribute to a decrease in menstrual bleeding. Additionally, ginkgo biloba creams may also help to stabilize the uterine lining and promote a healthy hormonal balance, thereby reducing the severity of heavy menstrual bleeding. This makes ginkgo biloba creams a natural and potential solution for women experiencing excessive menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene helps reduce bleeding by promoting the production of thromboxane A2, a compound that stimulates platelet aggregation and blood clotting.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions.

Isorhapontigenin: This lignan has estrogen-like properties that help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding by binding to estrogen receptors and modulating the activity of enzymes involved in the production of prostaglandins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their potential to reduce blood loss and alleviate menstrual cramps.
Buds: Used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and calm the uterus.
Seeds: Used for their potential to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Crush 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ginkgo powder with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler and heat at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Add 10 milliliters of jojoba oil and 5 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to 120 degrees fahrenheit before whipping with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the ginkgo cream to a clean container and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis creams helps with over bleeding during periods because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The root of the marshmallow plant, used in these creams, is rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the uterine lining. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Althaea officinalis creams may help to slow down or stop heavy bleeding during periods, providing relief for women who experience this issue. This natural remedy can offer a gentle and non-invasive solution for managing menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex carbohydrates help to form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and soothing the uterine lining to ease heavy menstrual bleeding.
Gallic acid: This phenolic compound has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing menstrual blood flow and alleviating excessive bleeding.

Glucuronic acid: As a component of mucilages, glucuronic acid helps to form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and soothing the uterine lining to ease heavy menstrual bleeding and prevent infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and protect the lining of the uterus, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms associated with menstrual cramps.
Barks: They possess anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried root in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes to make the herbal infusion.
Strain the infusion and discard the solids after letting it cool to room temperature within 30 minutes.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add the cooled herbal infusion to the beeswax mixture and stir until fully incorporated and melted within 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they work by regulating hormonal balance and stimulating uterine contractions. The cream's active compound, agnuside, interacts with the body's natural hormonal system, promoting a balance between estrogen and progesterone levels. This balance helps to reduce excessive bleeding by strengthening uterine contractions and promoting a more even menstrual cycle. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus creams may help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the uterine lining, further alleviating symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce menstrual bleeding by influencing hormonal balance and regulating the menstrual cycle.
Flavonoids: They contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate cramps and reduce excessive bleeding.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: These compounds may help regulate hormonal fluctuations and improve uterine health, leading to reduced menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their estrogen-regulating properties, which help balance hormonal imbalances that may cause heavy menstrual bleeding.
Seeds: Utilized for their hormonal regulating and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of hormonal imbalance.
Leaves: Employed for their ability to help regulate hormonal balance and reduce inflammation, contributing to a decrease in heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitex agnus-castus flowers in late summer when they are in full bloom and dry them completely.
Infuse 20 grams of dried flowers in 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 100 grams of shea butter and 50 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler.
Add 5 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain triterpenoid compounds that have a direct impact on the uterine muscles. These compounds help to relax the uterine walls, thereby reducing the intensity of menstrual flow. The anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra also help to soothe the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and promoting a balanced hormonal environment. This, in turn, helps to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate heavy bleeding during periods. The natural soothing effects of the cream also provide relief from menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding by modulating the prostaglandin levels in the body.
Flavonoids: These act as antioxidants and have a potential anti-inflammatory effect, which may help to reduce the frequency and severity of heavy menstrual bleeding.

Glycyrrhizic acid: This constituent has a potential effect on the uterine muscles, causing them to relax and reducing the frequency of contractions, which may help to alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and stabilize blood vessels.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used to make creams that help to tone and balance the hormonal levels, reducing excessive bleeding.
Barks: The bark of Glycyrrhiza glabra is used to make creams that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, alleviating menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier.
Steam distill the roots to obtain 10 grams of essential oil for 2 hours.
Mix 50 grams of emulsifying wax and 30 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Combine 10 grams of essential oil and 50 grams of aloe vera gel in a bowl.
Blend the oil and gel mixture with the wax mixture for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that help to reduce bleeding and promote clotting. The tannins in Hamamelis virginiana creams also help to constrict blood vessels, which can slow down the flow of blood and reduce the severity of bleeding. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding and is a popular alternative to synthetic medications. It works by promoting healthy coagulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Help with over bleeding during periods by contracting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to the affected area, which in turn reduces menstrual flow.
Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to excessive menstrual bleeding.
Saponins: Have astringent properties that help to contract and constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and alleviating menstrual cramps and excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in tannins and other compounds that help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding.
Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding and ease cramps.
Barks: They have antiseptic and astringent properties that help reduce bleeding and prevent infection in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in late summer and early fall for maximum potency and effectiveness.
Steep 1 ounce of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 to 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids then mix 2 tablespoons of the infused liquid with 2 tablespoons of distilled water.
Combine 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Blend the infused liquid mixture with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture to create a smooth and creamy texture.
